svg.title: The two-loop governance architecture
svg.desc: Vertical stack of three levels: User need, business strategy, and environment at top; How the system should work (shared mental model, system intent) in the middle; How the system actually works (runtime reality) at the bottom. Two bidirectional loops between adjacent levels. Loop 2 is Intent Alignment — calibrating the should-work model against user need and business strategy, served by OKRs, product reviews, customer feedback, and build-measure-learn cycles. Loop 1 is System Understanding — calibrating the should-work model against actual runtime behavior, served by tests, observability, code review, debugging, and post-mortems. Both loops ideally run continuously; AI as intelligence substrate participates in both — modeling system behavior alongside humans in Loop 1, and measuring and synthesizing user, market, and business signals in Loop 2.
svg.text: System intent is the pivot — continuously calibrated against reality below and need above

g.text: evolving needs, market signals, strategic priorities, competitive and regulatory reality
Does the should-work model still align with evolving user need and business strategy?
Served by OKRs, product reviews, customer feedback, build-measure-learn cycles
shared mental model and intent — captured in specs, ADRs, tests, contracts
Does the should-work model match how the system actually works?
Served by tests, observability, code review, debugging, post-mortems
runtime behavior, observed reality, what users actually experience

svg.text: Both loops ideally run continuously. AI as intelligence substrate participates in both — modeling system behavior alongside humans in Loop 1,
measuring and synthesizing user, market, and business signals in Loop 2 at cadences continuous calibration finally makes feasible.
